format_discipline.detail: No frontmatter — file opens directly with the '# NIST AI Risk Management Framework...' H1; no leading '---' YAML block. Sidecar reading.meta.json present beside reading.md, parses, and carries word_count (1395), estimated_read_minutes (7), citations_used ([1,2,3]), and sections. Fixed six sections present in exact order with no additions/renames/drops: Overview, Key Concepts, Worked Example, In Practice, Key Takeaways, References.

length.detail: word_count = 1395, inside the standard target_word_range [1200, 1600] and comfortably mid-band (not padded — corpus ran ~3% long at ~1650w; reading is a tightened pass below it). Reading-aloud minutes = 1395 / 230 = 6.07, inside target_minute_range [6, 8]. Earlier draft at 1258w (~5.5 min) would have undershot the 6-min floor on the words/230 sub-check; revised up by ~135 words of clarity (NIST-does-not-police note, fuller why-follow sentence, extra per-function examples) to clear it without padding beyond mid-band.

citations.detail: Primary resource [1] (NIST AI 100-1) cited heavily across Key Concepts and Key Takeaways. Supporting [2] (AIRC Core section) cited on each of the four-function descriptions and the loop; supporting [3] (NIST program page) cited on the voluntary / widely-adopted framing. Every primary resource cited at least once. Bibliography lists exactly [1][2][3], all three appear inline, no orphan markers and no orphan entries.

forward_references.detail: All cross-references point backward to earlier sequence positions: 5.1 (failure cases), 5.2 (hallucination), 5.3 (data bias), 5.4 (four pillars / accountability), 5.5 (red-teaming), 5.7 (EU AI Act). No mention of 5.11 / 5.12 / 5.13 or any later topic. Forward-reference discipline clean.

faithfulness.detail: Sampled 10 assertions — all trace to the corpus: (1) AI RMF is voluntary NIST guidance published Jan 2023; (2) NIST = National Institute of Standards and Technology, US agency, does not police; (3) risk = chance x severity; (4) four functions Govern/Map/Measure/Manage; (5) Govern cuts across the other three / soil analogy; (6) Map = draw the map before the journey; (7) Measure turns worry into evidence ('wrong 3x more often'); (8) Manage acts / human oversight / accept-and-document; (9) continuous loop not a checklist; (10) EU AI Act-vs-RMF contrast table. The resume-screening Worked Example is the corpus's own hands-on framing (chatbot/hiring screener; one sentence per function; re-check as a loop) rendered concrete — no new facts introduced. Zero unsupported assertions.

beginner_readability.detail: Audience = fresher-zero-background. Non-everyday terms defined bold-term / plain-meaning on first use: framework, NIST (acronym spelled out), AI RMF (acronym spelled out), voluntary, risk, function. red-teaming is reused as a named back-reference to 5.5 (prior concept), not re-defined cold. Concept-then-name order held (Govern 'is the soil', Map 'drawing the map before the journey'). No paragraph stacks 3+ brand-new terms. No hedging phrases. Short, active sentences addressed to 'you'. No cold/undefined terms.

formatting.detail: No paragraph exceeds ~4 sentences. The EU-Act-vs-RMF contrast is a table. The four functions are a bulleted definition set; the Worked Example is a numbered 4-step sequence; In Practice and Key Takeaways are bullets. Concrete inline examples sit beside each concept (policy/accountability under Govern, hiring tool under Map, group-disparity test under Measure, retrain/oversight under Manage) — not deferred solely to the Worked Example. A learner question is posed and answered inline ('So why follow guidance nobody forces on you? Because...'). Definitions use bold-term/plain-meaning shape.

assessment_references.detail: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights anywhere in the reading. The A1/A2 tie-in line carried by the gold examples (a flagged 2026-06-11 defect) was deliberately NOT replicated, per team decision. Clean.

scope.detail: On the headline: teaches the four functions (Govern, Map, Measure, Manage), the voluntary-vs-binding contrast, and the continuous-loop framing — exactly the topic title's slice at standard depth. No advanced NIST material leaked (no RMF Playbook subcategory detail, no AI 600-1 GenAI profile, no trustworthiness-characteristics deep-dive). Back-references (5.1-5.5, 5.7) appear as named cross-links to already-taught concepts, never re-taught. No scope_creep.

diagram_detail: diagram.png embedded inline in Key Concepts at the 'four functions as a continuous loop' subsection (corpus 5.3/5.8 spot) with a one-line caption, referenced via relative path ./diagram.png. Replaces structure prose rather than adding to it.
gold_example_note: Matched against .claude/references/gold-examples/reading/2-1-what-makes-a-good-specification.reading.md (standard-depth conceptual bar, ~1200w): same fixed-six sectioning, bold-term definitions, concrete-before-abstract per-function pattern, table for the X-vs-Y contrast, paragraphs <=4 sentences. Density is at the standard bar, not the deeper 3-4 bar. Correctly omits the gold examples' assessment tie-in line (the flagged defect).
verdict: PASS (cycle 1 of the critic loop, on the revised reading). All nine checks pass. Length 1395w is mid-band in [1200,1600] and 6.07 reading-aloud minutes clears the [6,8] floor; citations complete with no orphans; forward-reference discipline clean (no 5.11/5.12/5.13); faithful to corpus with zero unsupported assertions; beginner-readable with every new term defined before use; well chunked with a contrast table, a numbered worked example, and inline examples; no assessment references; on-headline with no scope creep. Diagram embedded inline at the continuous-loop spot with caption. No findings to escalate.
